title: isodon coetsa var. macraei
intro: Isodon coetsa var. macraei, also known as Himalayan isodon, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the Himalayas and is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, Isodon coetsa var. macraei is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being.
